name: Paddle (Merchant of Record Payments)
pricing_notes: 5% + $0.50 per transaction (no monthly fee); Merchant of Record model handles taxes

auth.notes: API key + client-side token; webhook signature verification

performance.notes: REST API v2; webhook-driven subscription events

agent_readiness.notes: Merchant of Record (MoR): Paddle handles VAT, GST, sales tax globally. from paddle_billing import Client. client = Client(api_key). client.products.create(name='Pro Plan', tax_category='saas'). client.prices.create(product_id=..., unit_price=...). Subscription management. No-code checkout. Paddle Billing (v2) is complete rewrite. Global tax compliance handled by Paddle. Digital products and SaaS. Popular for indie hackers and small SaaS (vs Stripe + TaxJar complexity).

gotchas: 5% + $0.50 fee is higher than Stripe 2.9% + $0.30 per transaction
Paddle Billing (v2) is complete rewrite — check SDK compatibility
Less flexibility than Stripe for complex payment flows
Funds held by Paddle (MoR) — payout schedule matters for cash flow
best_when: Digital products/SaaS needing global VAT/GST compliance without building it
Indie hackers and small teams wanting simple global payments
B2C SaaS where tax complexity (EU VAT, US sales tax) is a burden
When Merchant of Record liability shield is valuable
avoid_when: High transaction volumes (5% becomes expensive vs 2.9%)
summary: Paddle is a Merchant of Record (MoR) payments platform — handles global VAT, GST, sales tax for SaaS. Client(api_key).products.create().prices.create(). Subscription management, no-code checkout. 5% + $0.50 per transaction includes tax handling. Popular for indie hackers wanting to avoid tax complexity. Higher fees than Stripe but zero tax compliance burden. Paddle Billing v2 is major rewrite.
